title: if/else have incompatible types
explanation: This error occurs when the branches of an if/else expression return different types. In Rust, if/else is an expression and all branches must evaluate to the same type.
error_message: error[E0308]: `if` and `else` have incompatible types — expected `i32`, found `&str`

fixes.description: Make both branches return the same type
Use an enum to hold different types
Use a trait object for dynamic dispatch

tip: In Rust, if/else is an expression that produces a value. Both branches must return the same type, just like a function with multiple return paths.
